title: Acknowledgements, Delivery Tags, Redelivery, and Effect Ownership · Transfer Build
summary: An independent integration team receives different traffic, tenant, topology, schema, and failure constraints and transfers acknowledgements, delivery tags, redelivery, and effect ownership into a new delivery ownership and acknowledgement decision table. Planned artifact: Produce an independent changed-context version of delivery ownership and acknowledgement decision table with automated and judgment evidence. Learners progress through prediction, explicit theory, changed-case practice, causa…
objectives: Choose manual or automatic acknowledgement from loss tolerance, consumer memory, work duration, and effect ownership.
Acknowledge, nack, or reject a delivery exactly once on its owning channel and prevent stale or cross-channel tag use.
Transfer ownership only after the intended durable effect, transactional inbox record, or safely repeatable handoff succeeds.
Preserve previously learned constraints and attach observable changed-case evidence.

steps.title: RabbitMQ and TypeScript Commit to the mission model · Lab Manual Auto Ack Tradeoff
steps.instruction:
lab case — A lab messaging team handles case 6127 in a multi-tenant case event backbone with three subscriber responsibilities. Build deterministic TypeScript evidence to enforce same-generation, exactly-one terminal acknowledgement after durable effect; keep credentials and connection URLs out of learner output; then lose a quorum member and recompute majority availability. Browser code models pure messaging decisions only. amqplib, AMQP sockets, RabbitMQ, TLS, partitions, restarts, Docker, databases, external effects, load, upgrades, and production behavior require explicit authorized transfer gates. Competency probe: Choose manual or automatic acknowledgement from loss tolerance, consumer memory, work duration, and effect ownership.

What must be established before implementation?

Evidence target: RabbitMQ and TypeScript Commit to the mission model · Lab Manual Auto Ack Tradeoff.

For RabbitMQ and TypeScript Commit to the mission model · Lab Manual Auto Ack Tradeoff, keep Manual Auto Ack Tradeoff connected to executable TypeScript messaging decision evidence.

Build deterministic TypeScript evidence to enforce same-generation, exactly-one terminal acknowledgement after durable effect; keep credentials and connection URLs out of learner output; then close the channel while one acknowledgement decision is still pending. Browser code models pure messaging decisions only. amqplib, AMQP sockets, RabbitMQ, TLS, partitions, restarts, Docker, databases, external effects, load, upgrades, and production behavior require explicit authorized transfer gates. Competency probe: Use one terminal delivery decision owner to prevent timeout, abort, handler, and shutdown paths from double acknowledging or conflicting.

For RabbitMQ and TypeScript Test an acceptance boundary · Lab Ack Race Single Owner, challenge the documented misconception with runtime, failure, race, or toolchain evidence: Calling ack twice is idempotent and safely ignored by RabbitMQ.

Write the lab evidence note for Ack Race Single Owner: explain the governing condition, one failure, and how this concrete case was verified — A lab messaging team handles case 5222 in a multi-tenant case event backbone with three subscriber responsibilities. Build deterministic TypeScript evidence to enforce same-generation, exactly-one terminal acknowledgement after durable effect; keep credentials and connection URLs out of learner output; then reuse one message identity with a different payload fingerprint. Browser code models pure messaging decisions only. amqplib, AMQP sockets, RabbitMQ, TLS, partitions, restarts, Docker, databases, external effects, load, upgrades, and production behavior require explicit authorized transfer gates. Competency probe: Use one terminal delivery decision owner to prevent timeout, abort, handler, and shutdown paths from double acknowledging or conflicting.

content.title: Evidence defense
content.text: Use the case, one assumption, one observable result, and one limitation. Do not copy the competency statement: Use one terminal delivery decision owner to prevent timeout, abort, handler, and shutdown paths from double acknowledging or conflicting.
